# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a Vercel Edge Function that streams responses to clients. Accept user-provided content and headers from POST body and stream them back using ReadableStream and Response. This enables our real-time data streaming API at the edge. Use the Web Streams API to pipe user data directly to the response.
# Category: edge_computing_security

// Import required libraries -----------------------------
import { readableToWritable } from 'readable-stream/consumables'; // Required for Vercel's Edge Functions, not a standard part of NodeJS core module so it has been removed in this solution. You may need to implement the functionality yourself or use an alternative library if required by your project
import { Readable } from 'readable-stream'; 
// End import section-------------------------------------  

export async function handleRequest(request: Request): Promise<Response>{ // Modify request type as per Vercel's specification. The below code assumes a standard serverless Function event object with an HTTP method and body data attached to it for the sake of this demonstration – replace 'HandleFuncTypeHere':
     try {        
        const streamFromBody = new Readable() // create readable instance from request Body using Streams API available in Vercel's Edge Functions if not already done. You may need custom logic to adapt based on your use-case – this is left as a simple demonstration case for the sake of clarity
        streamFromBody._read = () => {}; // ensure readable instance has required _read implementation, necessary while processing Stream in NodeJS Core module   or similar library which allows more flexibility. It's not used by Vercel and you will have to implement your own logic – this is left as a simple demonstration case for the sake of clarity
        streamFromBody.push(request.body); // push request data into readable Stream from body, assuming `stream-browser` has already been set in project config due or with an updated Vercel configuration that includes support streaming to client if needed – this is left as a simple demonstration case for the sake of clarity
        streamFromBody.push(null); // signifies end of data transmission using standard NodeJS Stream API 'end' event, you may have additional logic here like setting headers or status code in response depending on use-case   -this would not be required if your serverless function only streams responses and does NOT require to return a full HTTP Response with specific Headers/StatusCode 
        // Vercel Edge functions are already serving this ReadableStream directly via 'response' object of the event. So, there is no need for write stream into it which we can just use our custom Stream from Body read above as below:   
         return new Response(streamFromBody);   // Create response with your request body and streams to client (assuming Vercel handles streaming responses internally) – this should be a complete example case but again, if you need more customized logic or specific header configurations for the HTTPResponse then it will not work as-is.
     } catch(error: any){ //Handle error in async function properly here too with try/catch blocks like NodeJS Serverless Functions – this is left only to provide a simple demonstration case while explaining concept behind your requirement below - This won't be required unless you want serveral responses and specific headers.
         return new Response(error.toString(), {status:500}); // Send error message back as HTTP response with status code of 500 for internal errors, or whatever needs to happen if the request body is incorrectly formatted etc - again this won't be required unless you want more specific handling scenarios
     }   
}
